questions.question: Why is one slot left unused in a typical array-based implementation of a circular queue? (2 marks)
keyPoints: To create an unambiguous distinction between the full and empty states.
If front equals rear, it could mean the queue is empty OR full. Sacrificing one slot resolves this ambiguity.

questions.question: Explain the operations of enqueue and dequeue in a circular queue with a suitable example. Draw the necessary diagrams to show the changes in the front and rear pointers. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Explanation of circular increment using modulo arithmetic.
Handling of the initial enqueue operation (setting front and rear to 0).
Handling of the dequeue operation that leads to an empty queue (resetting pointers to -1).
Step-by-step example trace with a diagram for at least 3 operations, including one wrap-around.
